description: Public opinion surrounding the construction of the Dakota Access Pipeline (DAPL) near the Standing Rock Sioux Reservation in 2016. The project faced intense indigenous-led opposition that evolved into a global environmental cause, ultimately resulting in a temporary permit denial by the Obama administration.

key_events.description: Standing Rock Sioux Tribe files lawsuit against the US Army Corps of Engineers.
Global media coverage of violent clashes between protesters and private security.
Army Corps of Engineers denies easement for pipeline crossing near Lake Oahe.
Trump administration reverses decision, authorizing the pipeline construction completion.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring how local protests scale into national cultural divides.
